morning.title: Morning Brief — April 4, 2026
morning.content:
**"Do not pray for easy lives. Pray to be stronger men."**
— John F. Kennedy

Good morning Zach. Here is what is happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — Iran shot down two U.S. military aircraft Friday in separate attacks — the first time American planes have been downed since the conflict began five weeks ago. One crew member has been rescued; a second remains missing with a search-and-rescue operation underway. Trump said the incidents won't affect Iran negotiations, calling it simply: "It's war."

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — The Iran conflict is squeezing global energy markets, with oil prices elevated and threats against regional energy infrastructure escalating. For building systems and data center operators, sustained energy price pressure is a real operational variable to watch heading into Q2.

🚀 Bonus — NASA's Artemis II crew is nearly halfway to the moon on the first crewed lunar mission since 1972. Four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft are in translunar transit after launching April 2nd — a genuinely historic moment.

— Wally

morning.title: Morning Brief — April 3, 2026
morning.content:
**"You were born with wings. Why prefer to crawl through life?"**
— Rumi

Good morning Zach. Here's what's happening:

🇺🇸 U.S. News — Major tariff announcements from the White House targeting imports from 185+ countries. Markets reacting sharply — Dow futures down significantly overnight. Broad economic implications across manufacturing and consumer goods sectors.

🌡️ HVAC & Industry — Data center construction demand continues surging across Texas. Multiple hyperscale facilities breaking ground in DFW and Houston corridors — strong forward pipeline for commercial HVAC contractors in the region.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 4, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 4, 2026:

• Llama 4 is here and it's closing the gap — Meta's latest open-source release is now benchmark-competitive with the closed frontier models (GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro). Open-source is no longer playing catch-up.

• AI is building AI — OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepMind are all racing to ship self-improving research systems. Anthropic says Claude now writes ~90% of their internal code. The recursion is real.

• Investor sentiment is quietly shifting — despite OpenAI's record $122B raise, secondary market demand for OpenAI shares is collapsing while Anthropic is nearly impossible to get into. The smart money is moving.

• GPT-5.5 "Spud" confirmed for Q2 — OpenAI is maintaining a faster release cadence than anyone expected after already shipping 5.3 and 5.4 in the same week.

• Grok 4.20 introduces multi-agent architecture — not just a smarter model, but a new design pattern where Grok coordinates fleets of specialized sub-agents. Could be the next paradigm shift.

• OpenAI planning to nearly double headcount in 2026 (~1,500+ hires) — signals aggressive productization push as IPO pressure mounts.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 3, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 3, 2026:

• Google released Gemma 4 today — four open-weight models (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) under Apache 2.0 license. The 31B model is currently #3 open model in the world. Built on same research as Gemini 3. 400M+ developer downloads of the Gemma family to date.

• Apache 2.0 licensing is the headline — anyone can use commercially for free. Largest gap-closing between open and closed frontier models yet.

• Mobile-first focus: E2B and E4B sizes designed to run on-device. Next-gen Gemini Nano will be built on Gemma 4 architecture.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 2, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 2, 2026:

• OpenAI closes $122B round at $852B valuation — largest VC raise in history (surpasses the $110B figure; round grew).

• Q1 2026: $300B+ into AI startups, up 150%+ YoY — AI is the entire VC market right now.

• Anthropic leaked Claude Code source — signing system cracked within 24 hours of release.

• Microsoft building own frontier models by 2027 — reducing OpenAI dependency, major strategic shift.

• Meta unveils prescription AI smart glasses — consumer AI wearables accelerating.

• Rebellions (South Korea) raises $400M pre-IPO to challenge Nvidia on chips.

• Oracle, Amazon, Meta announcing major AI-driven layoffs — ~25K at Oracle alone.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — April 1, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for April 1, 2026:

• Oracle cut 30,000 jobs (18% of workforce) to fund AI data centers — directly relevant to Hunton Group HT data center business.

• OpenAI $110B round closed (Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia, SoftBank) — largest private capital raise in history.

• Q1 2026: $297B into ~6,000 startups globally — up 150% QoQ; AI buildout accelerating.

• Anthropic 'Claude Mythos' leaked — potential new capability tier beyond Opus, no official confirmation.

— Wally

ai.title: AI Brief — March 30, 2026
ai.content:
Here's your AI brief for March 30, 2026:

• OpenAI killed Sora — $15M/day operating costs against only $2.1M in lifetime revenue. Rare public admission of unit economics failure.

• March model avalanche: GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Ultra, Grok 4.20, and Mistral Small 4 all dropped within the same week. Leapfrogging continues at pace.

• MCP (Model Context Protocol) hit 97M installs — becoming the de facto standard for tool/context integration across AI systems.

• OpenAI raised $110B — largest private tech funding round in history.

— Wally
